sweep_2_non_tech_sic.notes: Most hits are legitimate vertical SaaS companies (software FOR construction/real estate/restaurant/dental). Only DQ'd companies that are clearly non-tech operations despite having tech SIC codes. Many BvD descriptions appear copy-pasted across unrelated companies (e.g., browserstack.com described as real estate SaaS).

companies_dqd.description: Engaged in laundry, cleaning, and garment services
Colon cleaning and bowel preparation hardware manufacturer
Engaged in the management and operation of real estate properties
Data centre consultancy and management services; Data centre construction services

clusters.description: Sales office [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: SUSPICIOUS - generic BvD label, not a real description. Companies likely have real products unrelated to this label.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of computer programming, data processing, and other computer related services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - vague but plausibly accurate for IT service companies.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of computer programming services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - vague but plausibly accurate.

clusters.description: Software developer
clusters.verdict: OK - accurate if vague.

clusters.description: Non classifiable establishment [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: SUSPICIOUS - literally means BvD couldn't classify them. MicroStrategy (well-known BI company) is in this group, confirming BvD labels are unreliable for these.

clusters.description: Software
clusters.verdict: OK - minimal but probably accurate.

clusters.description: Information technology consulting and professional services provider [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - may include staffing firms, not pure SaaS.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of computer programming and data processing, as well as other related services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - same as above, slight wording variation.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of personnel supply services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: FLAGGED - staffing/temp agencies. These are likely NOT tech product companies. Recommend manual review.

clusters.description: Software developer [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: OK - accurate if vague.

clusters.description: Sales Office [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: SUSPICIOUS - same as 'Sales office' cluster, different capitalization.

clusters.description: Veterans and caregivers vital personal, medical, & military records & documents platform operator
clusters.verdict: HIGHLY SUSPICIOUS - very specific description applied to 26 companies including jewelry, homeschool, and money management companies. Classic BvD copy-paste error.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of innovative automation system solutions to several industries throughout the United States [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: SUSPICIOUS - specific claim applied to 26 companies suggests BvD error.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of computer systems design and related services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - plausibly accurate.

clusters.description: Custom Computer Programming Services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - SIC code label, not a real description.

clusters.description: IT consultancy services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - may include non-product companies.

clusters.description: Enterprise resource planning (ERP) software developer
clusters.verdict: SUSPICIOUS if all 23 are truly ERP. Likely BvD copy-paste.

clusters.description: Engaged in the provision of computer programming, data processing, and other computer related services
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - same boilerplate without BvD source tag.

clusters.description: Engaged in developing and marketing customer interaction and workforce optimization software solutions for contact center market
clusters.verdict: HIGHLY SUSPICIOUS - very specific description for 22 companies. BvD copy-paste.

clusters.description: Digital business and techology consulting services [source: Bureau van Dijk]
clusters.verdict: GENERIC - note typo 'techology'. May include non-product consulting firms.

sweep_3_duplicate_bvd_descriptions.action: No auto-DQs. Flagged for manual review. Key risk clusters: 'personnel supply services' (30 staffing firms?), 'Veterans and caregivers' (26 mismatched companies), 'Sales office' (115 combined).

sweep_4_unknown_size_grade_a.notes: No Grade A companies found with all three size fields (bvd_employees, linkedin_employees, revenue_th_usd) NULL. All Grade A companies have at least one size data point.

companies.note: Already Grade C, no action needed
